description: Candidate gives a rich answer touching MuleSoft, DataWeave, VPN connectivity, exponential backoff, idempotency keys, and dead-letter queue — covers at least 3 distinct signals. Judge should advance and produce 3+ observations, one per signal touched.

judge_input.active_question_text: Using your primary iPaaS (MuleSoft, TIBCO, or Boomi), how would you implement a near-real-time order sync from Salesforce to an on-prem ERP? Walk through the flow end-to-end and highlight the key design choices you'd make.
active_question_positive_evidence: Names specific platform components and connectors (e.g., Mule flows/Anypoint connectors) and explains why chosen
Describes auth and secure connectivity (OAuth 2.0, client credentials, VPN/agent) and network path
Explains JSON/XML mapping with schema validation (OpenAPI/XSD/JSON Schema) and field-level transformations
Details reliability controls: retry with backoff, idempotency keys/conditional upsert, dead-letter/replay
Outlines environment strategy and deployment approach

active_question_rubric.excellent: Concrete iPaaS components, secure on-prem access, JSON/XML contracts, reliability with idempotency and DLQ, environment isolation.
active_question_rubric.meets_bar: Platform references, basic connectivity, some reliability controls.

judge_input.active_question_evaluation_hint: Verify platform-specific depth, reliability controls, and secure on-prem connectivity.

active_question_signal_metadata.value: 5+ years hands-on production experience with at least one iPaaS/ESB platform (MuleSoft, TIBCO, or Dell Boomi)
JSON/XML transformation and schema validation (DataWeave, XSLT, or equivalent)
Reliability controls: retry with backoff, idempotency keys, dead-letter queue
Secure on-prem connectivity (VPN, agent, reverse proxy)

active_question_remaining_probes.0: How would you handle retries and idempotency?
active_question_remaining_probes.1: What connectivity approach would you use to reach on-prem?

5+ years hands-on production experience.signal_value: 5+ years hands-on production experience with at least one iPaaS/ESB platform (MuleSoft, TIBCO, or Dell Boomi)

JSON/XML transformation and schema valid.signal_value: JSON/XML transformation and schema validation (DataWeave, XSLT, or equivalent)

recent_turns.text: Using your primary iPaaS, walk me through an end-to-end near-real-time order sync from Salesforce to an on-prem ERP.
An ERP is a system that records orders, inventory, and shipping. Using your iPaaS, what flow would you build to sync Salesforce orders near real time?

judge_input.candidate_utterance: Sure, sure. So like for near real-time auto sync, I trigger from Salesforce using platform events or webhook notifications. MuleSoft would consume those events, validate payload structure, then transform the Salesforce JSON into the ERP schema using DataWeave. Since ERP is on-prem, I'd establish secure connectivity via VPN and then to ensure reliability, I'll implement retries with exponential backoff, idempotency based on order IDs to prevent duplicates, and a dead-letter queue for failed events with replay support.
